finding_results.notes: Inserted an on-page consequence beat at the pantry rupture: Ida breaks down in front of Lena with visible tears and a muffled sob, a church youth witnesses the fallout, and Lena directly acknowledges she hurt Ida by taking the key.

Kept one impact-simile template at the retained anchor ('The question landed like a slap') and rewrote other nearby 'felt ... like ...' constructions so the chapter no longer repeats the same formula in this scene family.
Reworked all four cited sensory beats to avoid the repeated exact "smell(ed) like" scaffold and varied perception through material texture and spatial pressure while staying Lena-close.
Replaced colon-labeled inventory fragments and verbless absence string with full subject-verb sentences, preserving the discovery cadence and clarity of what is present versus missing.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 2 now lands the family-cost beat in-scene with direct accountability from Lena, while preserving chapter continuity beats and reducing recurring metaphor-template repetition to a single retained instance.

Applied a focused prose/copyedit pass on chapter_02 that resolves both packet findings: recurring sensory-formula repetition was diversified, and clipped storage-inventory beats were rewritten into grammatical sentences without reducing scene pressure, voice specificity, or continuity.
